purpose: Operationalize the advisor's Issue-Manager / CI-ownership role map as a founder-gated DRAFT: SG decision addendum + NOOS operational binding + canonical role registry + NF-MOTOR-CI-REPAIR-001 recipe + route binding.
status: SUBMITTED for independent verification (author != verifier; no self-PASS)
custody_ordering_note: The custody chain is SG defines -> NOOS operationalizes -> runtime executes -> verifier proves. This is a role DEFINITION; SG ratifies it. The founder explicitly chose 'full operationalization draft', so NOOS drafted the operational artifacts ahead of ratification, but every artifact is marked PROPOSED / NOT_WIRED / SG_REVIEW_REQUIRED and is inert until SG ratifies + the founder merges. Nothing here is active authority.
l5_and_lock_disclosures: Recipe NF-MOTOR-CI-REPAIR-001 is an L5/founder-gated artifact by the registry's own rule ('the recipe diff IS the policy review'). Authored as a DRAFT PR = the sanctioned adoption path per motor/registry/docs/README.md.

test_motor_registry_invariants.py recipe-count assertion bumped 5 -> 6. This is an inventory assertion tracking a legitimately-added, schema-valid recipe — NOT a weakening of a verifier. No check, predicate, or pass-criterion was loosened; validate.py and all 52 invariant tests pass unchanged in logic.
The motor WIRING (data/noos-motor-executor-wiring-v1.json) was NOT touched. The route binding is NOT_WIRED (no machine owner) — NOOS classifies and stops; it does not auto-execute repair.
data/noos-canonical-roles-v1.json is a NEW registry (additive), carrying status PROPOSED_SG_REVIEW; it grants no active authority.

verification.activation_doctor: exit 0, 0 FAIL, 54 PASS, 2 STALE (noos_pin_freshness + sg_pin_freshness — pre-existing, unrelated: this branch is off main whose NOOS-pin refresh is in PR #81).

separation_of_duties_preserved: Issue Manager classifies/routes; SourceA compiles; Builder Owner repairs; CI Reliability Owner owns CI-system faults; CI verifies; founder approves promotion. No role classifies + patches + verifies + approves + merges its own work.
founder_decisions_surfaced: SG must ratify NF-UNIFIED-MOTOR-ISSUE-CI-OWNERSHIP-V1 (5 role IDs, capability-vs-agent framing, failure-class routing, recipe authority split, promotion authority).
Whether/when to WIRE the ci_failure_candidate_repair route (register a machine owner) — a separate later gate; kept NOT_WIRED here.
Confirm the recipe's authority split and 90-min / $15 / 2-retry budget are acceptable.

SoD concentration (surfaced by adversarial verification, non-blocking): portfolio-owner hosts BOTH issue-manager (classifier + closure confirmer) AND ci-reliability-owner (adjudicator for flaky/workflow/infra) — for non-candidate classes one identity classifies + adjudicates + confirms closure. Inert today (PROPOSED/NOT_WIRED) and both hats stripped of merge/deploy/bypass. SG should explicitly bless or split the dual hat, and require any future CI-Reliability remediation path to carry the same external-verifier + founder-gate discipline as the candidate-repair recipe before it is wired.
